key_insight: Artificial intelligence is positioned as the enabling layer that makes SKA-scale radio astronomy data manageable, interpretable, and scientifically productive.
engineering_relevance: For large-scale sensing systems, the paper shows how AI can automate real-time detection, calibration, scheduling, and anomaly handling under extreme data throughput.
method: Deep learning for source detection, RFI mitigation, anomaly detection, and parameter inference
Generative models for faster sky simulation, calibration, and imaging
Reinforcement learning and federated learning for autonomous control and distributed data workflows
evidence_signal: The abstract highlights petabyte-scale datasets and terabit-per-second data streams as the operational benchmark motivating AI methods across the SKA pipeline.
watch_item: Scientific deployment will depend on reliable explainability, uncertainty quantification, and physics-informed constraints to avoid opaque or untrustworthy decisions.
paper_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in the SKA Era
